# Night Watchman

Night Watchman is a self-hosted cron job output monitoring tool for file-artifact jobs. It checks whether scheduled jobs produced fresh, non-empty, correctly shaped outputs instead of trusting exit code 0 alone. Its AI investigation layer writes evidence dossiers with source pointers, while humans approve any bounded rerun or quarantine action. The product is free for up to 5 jobs, with a $39 one-time early-bird license for unlimited jobs through v1.x.

- Landing page: https://nightwatchman.dev/ — Product overview, demo, install command, pricing, and license link.
- What is a silent cron failure: https://nightwatchman.dev/what-is-a-silent-cron-failure.html — Defines jobs that exit 0 while producing empty, stale, or malformed artifacts.
- Exit code 0 is not a health check: https://nightwatchman.dev/exit-code-0-is-not-a-health-check.html — Explains why process success is not output correctness.
- Monitor AI-generated cron jobs: https://nightwatchman.dev/monitor-ai-generated-cron-jobs.html — Shows how to validate unattended scripts written or maintained by AI agents.
- Night Watchman vs Cronitor: https://nightwatchman.dev/night-watchman-vs-cronitor.html — Compares heartbeat monitoring with file-artifact validation.
- Night Watchman vs Healthchecks.io: https://nightwatchman.dev/night-watchman-vs-healthchecks.html — Explains how ping monitoring and artifact checks complement each other.
- FAQ: https://nightwatchman.dev/faq.html — Common questions about scope, AI usage, Telegram approval, pricing, and installation.
- Repository: https://github.com/ssddee-dev/code-batman — Source code, README, license, and installer.
- Demo video: https://youtu.be/ORbqkw7nBfU — Three-minute walkthrough of a silent cron failure and evidence escalation.
